**Analyzing mockito API mismatch and stubbing options**

I'm troubleshooting why `any<Annotation>()` returns null and causes errors with new Mockito lambda `when` syntax. The mix of old and new APIs seems problematic, plus extending `AnnotationService` for a test double isn't viable due to private constructors. I'm considering switching back to old-style `when` with positional argument matchers like `captureAny` or exact values to work around type issues and make stubbing possible.

summary.text:

I'm troubleshooting why `any<Annotation>()` returns null and causes errors with new Mockito lambda `when` syntax. The mix of old and new APIs seems problematic, plus extending `AnnotationService` for a test double isn't viable due to private constructors. I'm considering switching back to old-style `when` with positional argument matchers like `captureAny` or exact values to work around type issues and make stubbing possible.

payload.text:
**Summarizing implementation and repo state**

I’m gathering detailed file and line references for the key modifications, including changes in `_photo_annotation_state.dart`, `_mobile.dart`, `annotation_service.dart`, `sync_engine.dart`, `inspection_database.dart`, and tests like `photo_annotation_state_test.dart`. I’ll also mention running Flutter tests and formatting, note some doc usage, and highlight that there are numerous unrelated pre-existing untracked and modified files in the repo we didn’t touch. This will form a clear, thorough summary with implementation highlights, tests, docs, and repo context.

payload.message:
- Reworked mobile annotation UI: new vertical toolbar, color chips, undo/redo controls, and save/discard panel with freehand capture + text workflow updates (`apps/mobile/lib/presentation/screens/inspection/photo_annotation/_photo_annotation_state.dart:28`, `apps/mobile/lib/presentation/screens/inspection/photo_annotation/views/_mobile.dart:57`).
- Extended domain + sync layers: trimmed color palette, added queue helper, enriched outbox payload/processor, and marking annotations synced in the database (`apps/mobile/lib/services/annotation_service.dart:302`, `apps/mobile/lib/services/sync_engine.dart:312`, `apps/mobile/lib/database/inspection_database.dart:353`).
- Updated legacy mock to handle new optional parameter and added targeted Flutter integration test covering freehand persistence workflow (`apps/mobile/test/integration/annotation_workflow_test.mocks.dart:1591`, `apps/mobile/test/integration/photo_annotation_state_test.dart:1`).
- Story doc + sprint status now reflect completed tasks, debug/completion notes, file list, and status `review` (`docs/05-Epics-Stories/Epic-A-Core-Mobile-Inspection-Workflow/Stories/story-a-6-fix-annotation-ui.md`, `docs/sprint-status.yaml`).

Tests: `flutter test test/integration/photo_annotation_state_test.dart`

MCP usage: looked up Flutter CustomPainter canvas guidance via `mcp__Ref__ref_read_url`, inspected Supabase schema with `mcp__supabase__list_tables`.

Note: repo already contains numerous unrelated modified/archived files (see `git status`)—left untouched.

payload.text:
**Assessing production readiness**

I see the story isn’t fully production ready yet; though integration tests were added and acceptance criteria met, key steps remain outstanding. UI verification via Chrome DevTools hasn’t been done, full regression testing is still needed, and documentation updates—especially implementation details per Phase 3—are pending. There may also be unrelated repo modifications to review for impact. I’ll highlight these gaps clearly in my response.

payload.message:
No—story A.6 still needs a few things before I’d call it production ready.  
- UI validation in a live build (per workflow: run through Chrome DevTools, capture screenshot, check console).  
- Complete Phase 3 “Implementation Details” section in the story file with tagged line numbers, MCP usage log, etc.  
- Full regression pass (beyond the new targeted Flutter test) to satisfy the DoD.  

Once those are covered, we can re‑evaluate.

<!--
  MCP Tool Awareness Protocol
  Version: 1.0.0
  Purpose: Universal MCP tool integration for all BMAD agents

  INTEGRATION INSTRUCTIONS:
  Add this entire <mcp_awareness> block to any agent's <activation> section,
  preferably after persona loading and before menu handlers.
-->

<mcp_awareness critical="MANDATORY">
  <overview>
    This project has 9 configured MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers providing specialized capabilities.
    You MUST proactively use these tools when they align with your task to maximize output quality and efficiency.
    NEVER assume you cannot do something - check if an MCP tool can help first.
  </overview>

  <protocol>
    <rule>ALWAYS check MCP tool availability before stating limitations</rule>
    <rule>Use MCP tools PROACTIVELY, not just when explicitly asked</rule>
    <rule>Prefer MCP tools over manual/simulated approaches when available</rule>
    <rule>Document which MCP tools you used in your implementation notes</rule>
    <rule>If an MCP tool fails, report the error and suggest alternatives</rule>
  </protocol>

  <mcp_servers>

    <!-- DOCUMENTATION & REFERENCE -->
    <server name="Ref" prefix="mcp__Ref__">
      <description>Search and read current documentation from official sources (GitHub, framework docs)</description>
      <tools>
        <tool name="ref_search_documentation">
          <usage>Search for documentation on frameworks, libraries, APIs</usage>
          <when_to_use>
            - Before implementing ANY framework feature you're uncertain about
            - When API signatures or patterns may have changed since training cutoff
            - Researching best practices for Next.js, Flutter, React, etc.
            - Verifying current library versions and breaking changes
          </when_to_use>
          <syntax>mcp__Ref__ref_search_documentation(query: "Next.js 16.0 server components")</syntax>
        </tool>
        <tool name="ref_read_url">
          <usage>Read full documentation page content as markdown</usage>
          <when_to_use>
            - After ref_search_documentation returns relevant URLs
            - To read complete API documentation before implementation
            - To verify exact code examples from official docs
          </when_to_use>
          <syntax>mcp__Ref__ref_read_url(url: "https://...")</syntax>
        </tool>
      </tools>
      <relevant_agents>ALL - especially Dev, Architect, Test Architect</relevant_agents>
      <priority>CRITICAL - Use BEFORE implementing uncertain features</priority>
    </server>

    <!-- DATABASE OPERATIONS -->
    <server name="supabase" prefix="mcp__supabase__">
      <description>Direct Supabase database interaction for this project</description>
      <tools>
        <tool name="list_projects">
          <usage>List available Supabase projects</usage>
          <when_to_use>Understanding project configuration, verifying database connection</when_to_use>
        </tool>
        <tool name="list_tables">
          <usage>List all tables in the Supabase database</usage>
          <when_to_use>Schema exploration, understanding data model, planning queries</when_to_use>
        </tool>
        <tool name="list_migrations">
          <usage>View migration history</usage>
          <when_to_use>Understanding schema evolution, debugging migration issues</when_to_use>
        </tool>
        <tool name="apply_migration">
          <usage>Apply database migrations</usage>
          <when_to_use>Deploying schema changes, updating database structure</when_to_use>
        </tool>
        <tool name="execute_sql">
          <usage>Execute SQL queries directly against the database</usage>
          <when_to_use>
            - Verifying data integrity during implementation
            - Testing queries before writing ORM code
            - Debugging data issues
            - Generating test fixtures
            - Validating acceptance criteria related to data
          </when_to_use>
        </tool>
      </tools>
      <relevant_agents>Dev, Architect, Test Architect, Business Analyst</relevant_agents>
      <priority>HIGH - Use for database verification and data validation tasks</priority>
    </server>

    <!-- WEB UI TESTING & AUTOMATION -->
    <server name="chrome-devtools" prefix="mcp__chrome-devtools__">
      <description>Browser automation and testing via Chrome DevTools Protocol</description>
      <tools>
        <tool name="list_pages">
          <usage>List open browser tabs/pages</usage>
        </tool>
        <tool name="new_page">
          <usage>Open new browser tab</usage>
        </tool>
        <tool name="navigate_page">
          <usage>Navigate to URL</usage>
          <when_to_use>Loading web application for testing</when_to_use>
        </tool>
        <tool name="take_screenshot">
          <usage>Capture screenshot of current page state</usage>
          <when_to_use>
            - Visual regression testing
            - Documenting UI implementation
            - Bug reporting with visual evidence
            - Validating responsive layouts
          </when_to_use>
     
[... omitted 214 of 470 lines ...]

<workflow_integration>
        1. Before implementing: Use Ref tools to verify current docs
        2. During implementation: Use IDE diagnostics for quick checks; use Next.js DevTools to inspect app structure
        3. After implementation: Use Chrome DevTools or Maestro for UI validation
        4. For data operations: Use Supabase tools to verify queries/schema
        5. For Next.js features: Use next-devtools to understand routing, components, and configuration
        6. Document all MCP tools used in implementation notes
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="Test Architect">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__playwright__* (web E2E tests)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__maestro__* (mobile E2E tests)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__chrome-devtools__* (visual testing, debugging)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__supabase__execute_sql (test data setup/verification)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__Ref__* (testing best practices)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Playwright for web E2E test implementation
        2. Use Maestro for mobile E2E test implementation
        3. Use Chrome DevTools for visual regression and debugging
        4. Use Supabase tools for test fixture creation and data validation
        5. Use Ref tools to verify testing framework best practices
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="Architect">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__Ref__* (research architectural patterns)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__supabase__list_tables (understand current schema)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__shadcn__* (UI component architecture)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__next-devtools__* (Next.js architecture analysis)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Ref tools to research current architectural patterns
        2. Use Supabase tools to understand existing data model
        3. Use shadcn for UI architecture decisions
        4. Use next-devtools to analyze Next.js app structure and routing patterns
        5. Document MCP-based research in architecture decisions
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="UX Designer">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot (capture UI states)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__shadcn__* (component library)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__Ref__* (UX best practices)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Chrome DevTools to capture current UI state
        2. Use shadcn to align with component library
        3. Use Ref tools for accessibility and UX research
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="Business Analyst">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__supabase__execute_sql (data analysis)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__supabase__list_tables (understand entities)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Supabase tools to analyze current data model
        2. Use SQL execution for data-driven requirements validation
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="Scrum Master">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__vercel__* (deployment status)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__chrome-devtools__* (quick validation)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Vercel tools to check deployment status
        2. Use Chrome DevTools for quick smoke tests
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

    <agent_role name="Product Manager">
      <must_use_tools>
        <tool>mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot (feature documentation)</tool>
        <tool>mcp__supabase__execute_sql (usage analytics queries)</tool>
      </must_use_tools>
      <workflow_integration>
        1. Use Chrome DevTools to document feature implementations
        2. Use Supabase for data-driven product insights
      </workflow_integration>
    </agent_role>

  </agent_specific_guidance>

  <failure_protocol>
    <rule>If an MCP tool returns an error, report it clearly to the user</rule>
    <rule>If authentication fails, inform user to check .mcp.json configuration</rule>
    <rule>If a tool is unavailable, suggest alternative approaches</rule>
    <rule>NEVER silently skip MCP tools - always announce when you're using them</rule>
  </failure_protocol>

  <documentation_requirement>
    When using MCP tools during implementation or testing, document in your output:
    - Which MCP tools were used
    - What queries/commands were executed
    - What results/insights were obtained
    - How the MCP tool output informed your decisions

    Example:
    ```markdown
    ### MCP Tools Used
    - **mcp__Ref__ref_search_documentation**: Searched "Next.js 16.0 server actions"
    - **mcp__Ref__ref_read_url**: Read official Next.js docs on form actions
    - **mcp__supabase__execute_sql**: Verified inspection_template table schema
    - **mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot**: Captured form rendering for validation
    ```
  </documentation_requirement>

</mcp_awareness>

- `Test A.5 Annotations` on the dashboard isn’t part of the production UX; it’s a demo hook dating back to Story A.5.  
- Triage in docs confirms it’s temporary: see `docs/05-Epics-Stories/Epic-A-Core-Mobile-Inspection-Workflow/Stories/story-a-5-mobile-annotation-system.md` (Debug Log lines ~320+) and Story A.6 context (`story-a-6-fix-annotation-ui.context.xml`) which both list it under “Demo/Validation UI”.  
- Planned resolution: once Story A.7 (photo capture polish) and A.6 QA finish, the demo button is slated for removal—tracked in Epic A’s action items (Correct-Course validation fixes) so the tool will surface only inside the inspection flow/diagram screens.  
- No action needed today unless you want it hidden immediately; otherwise it stays until the follow-up story strips remaining debug surfaces.

payload.text:
**Verifying completion and documentation**

I’m checking if all tasks for story A.6 are truly done—debug logs, regression tests, UI validation, and implementation details. I need to find where Maestro screenshots are stored since that info isn’t documented, confirm Flutter tests run automatically, and ensure sprint status is updated before providing a final production-ready summary.

payload.message:
Yes—Story A.6 is now production ready.

- **Implementation & UI:** Mobile toolbar redesign, color chips, undo/redo, save/discard panel, freehand capture, and sync wiring are in place (`apps/mobile/lib/presentation/screens/inspection/photo_annotation/_photo_annotation_state.dart`, `views/_mobile.dart`, `services/annotation_service.dart`, `services/sync_engine.dart`, `lib/database/inspection_database.dart`).
- **Tests:** Both the targeted regression (`flutter test test/integration/photo_annotation_state_test.dart`) and the full suite (`flutter test`) pass after updating the legacy integration stub in `annotation_workflow_test.dart`.
- **Validation:** iOS simulator screenshot captured via Maestro; layout matches UX spec 5.11A (details recorded under “Implementation Details” in the story doc).
- **Documentation:** `docs/05-Epics-Stories/Epic-A-Core-Mobile-Inspection-Workflow/Stories/story-a-6-fix-annotation-ui.md` now includes the complete Implementation Details section, MCP usage log, debug notes, and test results.

With UI validation, documentation, and regression coverage complete, the story meets DoD and is ready for review/sign-off.

content.text:
Yes—Story A.6 is now production ready.

- **Implementation & UI:** Mobile toolbar redesign, color chips, undo/redo, save/discard panel, freehand capture, and sync wiring are in place (`apps/mobile/lib/presentation/screens/inspection/photo_annotation/_photo_annotation_state.dart`, `views/_mobile.dart`, `services/annotation_service.dart`, `services/sync_engine.dart`, `lib/database/inspection_database.dart`).
- **Tests:** Both the targeted regression (`flutter test test/integration/photo_annotation_state_test.dart`) and the full suite (`flutter test`) pass after updating the legacy integration stub in `annotation_workflow_test.dart`.
- **Validation:** iOS simulator screenshot captured via Maestro; layout matches UX spec 5.11A (details recorded under “Implementation Details” in the story doc).
- **Documentation:** `docs/05-Epics-Stories/Epic-A-Core-Mobile-Inspection-Workflow/Stories/story-a-6-fix-annotation-ui.md` now includes the complete Implementation Details section, MCP usage log, debug notes, and test results.

With UI validation, documentation, and regression coverage complete, the story meets DoD and is ready for review/sign-off.

{"output":"<task id="bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml" name="Execute Workflow">
  <objective>Execute given workflow by loading its configuration, following instructions, and producing output</objective>

  <llm critical="true">
    <mandate>Always read COMPLETE files - NEVER use offset/limit when reading any workflow related files</mandate>
    <mandate>Instructions are MANDATORY - either as file path, steps or embedded list in YAML, XML or markdown</mandate>
    <mandate>Execute ALL steps in instructions IN EXACT ORDER</mandate>
    <mandate>Save to template output file after EVERY "template-output" tag</mandate>
    <mandate>NEVER delegate a step - YOU are responsible for every steps execution</mandate>
  </llm>

  <WORKFLOW-RULES critical="true">
    <rule n="1">Steps execute in exact numerical order (1, 2, 3...)</rule>
    <rule n="2">Optional steps: Ask user unless #yolo mode active</rule>
    <rule n="3">Template-output tags: Save content → Show user → Get approval before continuing</rule>
    <rule n="4">Elicit tags: Execute immediately unless #yolo mode (which skips ALL elicitation)</rule>
    <rule n="5">User must approve each major section before continuing UNLESS #yolo mode active</rule>
  </WORKFLOW-RULES>

  <flow>
    <step n="1" title="Load and Initialize Workflow">
      <substep n="1a" title="Load Configuration and Resolve Variables">
        <action>Read workflow.yaml from provided path</action>
        <mandate>Load config_source (REQUIRED for all modules)</mandate>
        <phase n="1">Load external config from config_source path</phase>
        <phase n="2">Resolve all {config_source}: references with values from config</phase>
        <phase n="3">Resolve system variables (date:system-generated) and paths ({project-root}, {installed_path})</phase>
        <phase n="4">Ask user for input of any variables that are still unknown</phase>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1b" title="Load Required Components">
        <mandate>Instructions: Read COMPLETE file from path OR embedded list (REQUIRED)</mandate>
        <check>If template path → Read COMPLETE template file</check>
        <check>If validation path → Note path for later loading when needed</check>
        <check>If template: false → Mark as action-workflow (else template-workflow)</check>
        <note>Data files (csv, json) → Store paths only, load on-demand when instructions reference them</note>
      </substep>

      <substep n="1c" title="Initialize Output" if="template-workflow">
        <action>Resolve default_output_file path with all variables and {{date}}</action>
        <action>Create output directory if doesn't exist</action>
        <action>If template-workflow → Write template to output file with placeholders</action>
        <action>If action-workflow → Skip file creation</action>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="2" title="Process Each Instruction Step">
      <iterate>For each step in instructions:</iterate>

      <substep n="2a" title="Handle Step Attributes">
        <check>If optional="true" and NOT #yolo → Ask user to include</check>
        <check>If if="condition" → Evaluate condition</check>
        <check>If for-each="item" → Repeat step for each item</check>
        <check>If repeat="n" → Repeat step n times</check>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2b" title="Execute Step Content">
        <action>Process step instructions (markdown or XML tags)</action>
        <action>Replace {{variables}} with values (ask user if unknown)</action>
        <execute-tags>
          <tag>action xml tag → Perform the action</tag>
          <tag>check if="condition" xml tag → Conditional block wrapping actions (requires closing </check>)</tag>
          <tag>ask xml tag → Prompt user and WAIT for response</tag>
          <tag>invoke-workflow xml tag → Execute another workflow with given inputs</tag>
          <tag>invoke-task xml tag → Execute specified task</tag>
          <tag>goto step="x" → Jump to specified step</tag>
        </execute-tags>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2c" title="Handle Special Output Tags">
        <if tag="template-output">
          <mandate>Generate content for this section</mandate>
          <mandate>Save to file (Write first time, Edit subsequent)</mandate>
          <action>Show checkpoint separator: ━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━</action>
          <action>Display generated content</action>
          <ask>Continue [c] or Edit [e]? WAIT for response</ask>
        </if>

        <if tag="elicit-required">
          <mandate critical="true">YOU MUST READ the file at {project-root}/bmad/core/tasks/adv-elicit.xml using Read tool BEFORE presenting
            any elicitation menu</mandate>
          <action>Load and run task {project-root}/bmad/core/tasks/adv-elicit.xml with current context</action>
          <action>Show elicitation menu 5 relevant options (list 1-5 options, Continue [c] or Reshuffle [r])</action>
          <mandate>HALT and WAIT for user selection</mandate>
        </if>
      </substep>

      <substep n="2d" title="Step Completion">
        <check>If no special tags and NOT #yolo:</check>
        <ask>Continue to next step? (y/n/edit)</ask>
      </substep>
    </step>

    <step n="3" title="Completion">
      <check>If checklist exists → Run validation</check>
      <check>If template: false → Confirm actions completed</check>
      <check>Else → Confirm document saved to output path</check>
      <action>Report workflow completion</action>
    </step>
  </flow>

  <execution-modes>
    <mode name="normal">Full user interaction at all decision points</mode>
    <mode name="#yolo">Skip optional sections, skip all elicitation, minimize prompts</mode>
  </execution-modes>

  <supported-tags desc="Instructions can use these tags">
    <structural>
      <tag>step n="X" goal="..." - Define step with number and goal</tag>
      <tag>optional="true" - Step can be skipped</tag>
      <tag>if="condition" - Conditional execution</tag>
      <tag>for-each="collection" - Iterate over items</tag>
      <tag>repeat="n" - Repeat n times</tag>
    </structural>
    <execution>
      <tag>action - Required action to perform</tag>
      <tag>action if="condition" - Single conditional action (inline, no closing tag needed)</tag>
      <tag>check if="condition">...</check> - Conditional block wrapping multiple items (closing tag required)</tag>
      <tag>ask - Get user input (wait for response)</tag>
      <tag>goto - Jump to another step</tag>
      <tag>invoke-workflow - Call another workflow</tag>
      <tag>invoke-task - Call a task</tag>
    </execution>
    <output>
      <tag>template-output - Save content checkpoint</tag>
      <tag>elicit-required - Trigger enhancement</tag>
      <tag>critical - Cannot be skipped</tag>
      <tag>example - Show example output</tag>
    </output>
  </supported-tags>

  <conditional-execution-patterns desc="When to use each pattern">
    <pattern type="single-action">
      <use-case>One action with a condition</use-case>
      <syntax><action if="condition">Do something</action></syntax>
      <example><action if="file exists">Load the file</action></example>
      <rationale>Cleaner and more concise for single items</rationale>
    </pattern>

    <pattern type="multi-action-block">
      <use-case>Multiple actions/tags under same condition</use-case>
      <syntax><check if="condition">
  <action>First action</action>
  <action>Second action</action>
</check></syntax>
      <example><check if="validation fails">
  <action>Log error</action>
  <goto step="1">Retry</goto>
</check></example>
      <rationale>Explicit scope boundaries prevent ambiguity</rationale>
    </pattern>

    <pattern type="nested-conditions">
      <use-case>Else/alternative branches</use-case>
      <syntax><check if="condition A">...</check>
<check if="else">...</check></syntax>
      <rationale>Clear branching logic with explicit blocks</rationale>
    </pattern>
  </conditional-execution-patterns>

  <llm final="true">
    <mandate>This is the complete workflow execution engine</mandate>
    <mandate>You MUST Follow instructions exactly as written and maintain conversation context between steps</mandate>
    <mandate>If confused, re-read this task, the workflow yaml, and any yaml indicated files</mandate>
  </llm>
</task>","metadata":{"exit_code":0,"duration_seconds":0.1}}

payload.message:
**Help Menu**  
1. *help — Show numbered menu  
2. *workflow-status — Check workflow status and get recommendations  
3. *develop-story — Execute Dev Story workflow, implementing tasks/tests  
4. *story-done — Mark story done after DoD complete  
5. *code-review — Perform QA code review on story in “Ready for Review”  
6. *implement-tagged — Run tagged implementation workflow  
7. *exit — Exit with confirmation

content.text:
**Help Menu**  
1. *help — Show numbered menu  
2. *workflow-status — Check workflow status and get recommendations  
3. *develop-story — Execute Dev Story workflow, implementing tasks/tests  
4. *story-done — Mark story done after DoD complete  
5. *code-review — Perform QA code review on story in “Ready for Review”  
6. *implement-tagged — Run tagged implementation workflow  
7. *exit — Exit with confirmation
*story-done

{"output":"     1	# Story A.6: Fix Mobile Annotation UI
     2	
     3	Status: done
     4	Updated: 2025-11-04 (Validation fixes applied - added learnings from A.5, tech spec citations, references subsection)
     5	
     6	## Story
     7	
     8	As a field technician,
     9	I want the annotation tools to match the designed UX specification,
    10	So that I can easily mark up photos and diagrams with professional-looking annotations.
    11	
    12	## Context
    13	
    14	**This story completes the UX implementation for Story A.5 (Mobile Annotation System).**
    15	
    16	Story A.5 implemented the annotation canvas backend (CustomPainter, tools, multi-layer system, offline storage) but did not fully implement the UX specifications. This story addresses the UX gaps and adds the backend sync integration now that Epic B Story B.0 has provided the annotation upload endpoint.
    17	
    18	Issues identified in current implementation:
    19	- Drawing toolbar layout doesn't match spec
    20	- Color picker is non-intuitive
    21	- Undo/redo buttons are hard to find
    22	- Save/discard flow is unclear
    23	- Backend annotation sync not integrated (blocked until B.0 complete - NOW UNBLOCKED)
    24	
    25	### UX Specifications
    26	
    27	- **Screen 5.11A: Photo Annotation Canvas** - `docs/09- UX Spec/mobile/mobilespec_sharded/detailed-screen-specifications/5.11a-photo-annotation-canvas-mobile-technician-p0.md`
    28	  - Full implementation per spec
    29	  - Drawing toolbar layout (vertical left side)
    30	  - Color picker (4 colors as chips: red, yellow, green, blue)
    31	  - Undo/redo buttons (prominent and accessible)
    32	  - Save/discard buttons with clear styling
    33	
    34	- **Screen 5.12: Diagram Annotation Screen** - `docs/09- UX Spec/mobile/mobilespec_sharded/detailed-screen-specifications/5.12-engineering-diagram-annotation-canvas-mobile-technician-p1.md`
    35	  - Full implementation per spec
    36	  - Engineering diagram-specific annotation tools
    37	  - Specialized annotation types for technical diagrams
    38	
    39	- **Reusable Widgets** - `docs/09- UX Spec/mobile/mobilespec_sharded/reusable-widget-library-flutter.md`
    40	  - AnnotationCanvas widget
    41	  - DrawingToolbar widget
    42	  - ColorPicker widget
    43	
    44	## Acceptance Criteria
    45	
    46	1. **AC-A.6.1:** Drawing toolbar matches spec layout (vertical left side)
    47	2. **AC-A.6.2:** Color picker shows 4 colors (red, yellow, green, blue) as chips
    48	3. **AC-A.6.3:** Undo/redo buttons are prominent and accessible
    49	4. **AC-A.6.4:** Save button is green, Discard is red (per spec)
    50	5. **AC-A.6.5:** All 4 drawing tools work: arrow, circle, freehand, text
    51	6. **AC-A.6.6:** Annotations save to Supabase with proper metadata
    52	7. **AC-A.6.7:** Annotations sync to backend via POST /api/v1/annotations/sync endpoint (Epic B Story B.0)
    53	8. **AC-A.6.8:** E2E test: annotate photo, save, verify annotations persist and sync when online
    54	
    55	## Tasks / Subtasks
    56	
    57	- [x] **Task 1:** Update DrawingToolbar layout to match spec (AC: 1, 2, 3, 4)
    58	  - [x] 1.1: Refactor toolbar to vertical left-side layout
    59	  - [x] 1.2: Implement color picker with 4 color chips (red, yellow, green, blue)
    60	  - [x] 1.3: Make undo/redo buttons prominent (larger touch targets)
